level_signals: 4+ years of experience in the software industry
Expected to own full end-to-end product experience across multiple core product areas
Expected to become one of the most credible subject matter experts on the team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of experience in the software industry (ideally as a product manager or engineer)
must_haves.qualifier: ideally as a product manager or engineer

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Significant experience building 0-1 enterprise software in regulated industries
Ability and interest to dig into mortgage and build a deep understanding of the full value chain
Experience conducting user research using a variety of methods
Ability to run SQL queries to analyze user data and identify trends and insights
Experience creating wireframes and workflow diagrams using Figma or similar tools
Strong design sense and ability to work closely with designers to build high-quality products
Ability to effectively manage strategic partners
Must live in or be willing to relocate to Palo Alto (CA) or New York City.
Leverage AI and LLMs in both your daily workflow (specs, designs, etc.), but also as a meaningful part of your solution space to solve problems
Have strong, principled opinions that you're willing to defend. We have a culture of vigorous discussion and debate on decisions.
Are known to ruthlessly prioritize and maintain organizational focus.
Comfortable operating in highly regulated markets whilst optimizing for performance.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working on mortgage products and/or fintech infrastructure strongly preferred

scope.surface: Full end-to-end mortgage origination lifecycle platform — APIs, Decisioning, Capital, Compliance, Command Center (dashboard), Elements (UI building blocks for borrower-facing experiences)
scope.business_model: B2B API/infrastructure platform for mortgage originators
primary_metrics_inferred: Speed and scale of mortgage originations on the platform
Automation of manual backend mortgage processes
core_responsibilities: Own the full end-to-end experience throughout the mortgage origination lifecycle
Leverage applied AI (document extraction, optimizers, MCP servers) in core product experiences
Help onboard and manage 3rd party integrations
Be a thought partner and build a strong feedback loop with engineering
Spec, build, and launch support for new loan products
Support GTM and Account Management teams in the deal process and customer success
Surface backend process issues, lead long-term solution discussions, and manage temporary manual workarounds

green_flags: Clear product surface with named areas (APIs, Decisioning, Capital, Compliance, Command Center, Elements)
Well-known investor backing (Peter Thiel, QED, Citi, Fifth Wall, founders of Ramp, Blend, Mercury)
Revenue described as compounding monthly — signals product-market traction
Explicitly states 'we don't expect you to be an expert in mortgage on day 1' — acknowledges learning curve
Strong AI-native product mandate (LLMs, MCP servers, document extraction) — forward-looking scope
red_flags: Relocation required to Palo Alto or NYC — hard constraint that eliminates remote candidates entirely
Extremely high bar: expected to 'become one of the most credible and reliable subject matter experts' in mortgage
Startup intensity signals ('not for those seeking comfort', 'former founders', 'move quickly') may indicate unsustainable pace
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization — unclear for international candidates
Equity and total comp entirely absent from JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description as B2B API platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and company mission; not stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite from relocation requirement; not explicitly labeled)
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred as false; equity not mentioned anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: JD says '4+ years of experience in the software industry (ideally as a product manager or engineer)' — the parenthetical 'ideally' softens the PM/engineer framing, but the 4+ year floor is stated as a hard requirement under 'Requirements'. Treated as hard.

The phrase 'strongly preferred' for mortgage/fintech experience creates ambiguity — it is stronger than 'nice to have' but falls short of a hard requirement. Classified as nice_to_have with soft severity.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Marked as 'unclear'.
